userStory: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials, so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
applicationContext: WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
entryConditions: Verify the user is unauthenticated before starting the workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials, so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins at the exact URL:
The Login page is not directly accessed by URL in this workflow and must be opened through the Home page person/profile button.
Successful authentication returns the user to `home_page` in authenticated state.
behaviorRules: The Login page must be opened through the `home_page` person/profile button for this workflow.
Password input must be masked by default.
Valid credentials must authenticate the user successfully.
Invalid credentials must not authenticate the user.
Authentication failures, including blank username, blank password, partial credentials, and incorrect credentials, must display only the generic `Failed` message.
Separate required-field validation messaging must not be displayed.
Failed authentication must maintain unauthenticated state.
Leading and trailing whitespace handling must follow implemented application behavior and be observationally validated.
Back and Home buttons on the Login page must return the user to `home_page` without authentication.
Successful authentication must return the user to `home_page` in authenticated state.
validationExpectations: The Login page opens successfully from `home_page`.
Username, Password, and Login controls are visible and interactive.
Back and Home buttons are visible and functional.
Password input remains masked during entry.
Invalid credential submissions display the generic `Failed` message.
Blank credential submissions display the generic `Failed` message.
Partial credential submissions display the generic `Failed` message.
No dedicated required-field validation message is displayed.
Failed authentication does not transition the application into authenticated state.
Successful authentication using approved credentials returns the user to authenticated `home_page`.
Authenticated user identity and authenticated-only features are visible after successful login.
Whitespace-modified credential handling is validated according to implemented application behavior.
transitionExpectations: Clicking the person/profile button transitions the application from `home_page` in `guest` state to the Login page.
Successful authentication transitions the application from the Login page to `home_page` in authenticated state.
Failed authentication keeps the user on the Login page or otherwise maintains unauthenticated state.
Invalid, blank, partial, and whitespace-modified credential failures must not transition the application into authenticated state.
Back button navigation returns the user from the Login page to `home_page` in unauthenticated state.
Home button navigation returns the user from the Login page to `home_page` in unauthenticated state.
Independent negative and return-navigation branches must begin from the canonical `home_page` `guest` entry context.
The unauthenticated entry state must be restored before executing each independent branch.
Transition from `home_page` to the Login page.
Validate Login page controls and behaviors.
Submit authentication using the Login button.
reuseGuidance: Reuse the existing Login page resource for:

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Login page controls are visible and interactive after navigation from home_page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button are visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Login page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify password input remains masked while typing credentials
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Password field masks the entered password characters during input and the raw password value is not visibly displayed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify successful authentication using approved credentials returns the user to authenticated home_page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The user is authenticated successfully, the application returns to home_page in authenticated state, authenticated user identity is displayed, and authenticated-only UI features become visible.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify invalid username and invalid password display only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message is shown, and the application remains unauthenticated on the Login page or equivalent unauthenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify blank Username and blank Password submission displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation message appears, and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify submission with only Username populated displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed without dedicated required-field validation messaging, and the application remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify submission with only Password populated displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed without dedicated required-field validation messaging, and the application remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify incorrect password for approved username does not authenticate the user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, authenticated user identity is not shown, and the application remains in unauthenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify leading whitespace in Username credential is observationally validated
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application behavior for leading whitespace in credentials is consistently observable, and if authentication fails the user remains unauthenticated with only the generic Failed message displayed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify trailing whitespace in Password credential is observationally validated
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application behavior for trailing whitespace in credentials is consistently observable, and failed authentication does not transition the application into authenticated state while displaying only the generic Failed message.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify whitespace-only credential values do not authenticate the user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation appears, and the application remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button returns the user to home_page without authentication
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state and authenticated-only UI is not visible.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button returns the user to home_page without authentication
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state and authenticated-only features are not displayed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify failed authentication does not expose authenticated user identity or authenticated-only features
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application remains unauthenticated, authenticated user identity is not displayed, authenticated-only features remain inaccessible, and only the generic Failed message is shown.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Login button processes pasted approved credentials successfully
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The pasted approved credentials authenticate successfully and the application returns to authenticated home_page with authenticated user identity and authenticated-only UI visible.

validations: The Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button are visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Login page.
The Password field masks the entered password characters during input and the raw password value is not visibly displayed.
The user is authenticated successfully, the application returns to home_page in authenticated state, authenticated user identity is displayed, and authenticated-only UI features become visible.

Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message is shown, and the application remains unauthenticated on the Login page or equivalent unauthenticated state.
Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation message appears, and the user remains unauthenticated.
Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed without dedicated required-field validation messaging, and the application remains unauthenticated.
Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, authenticated user identity is not shown, and the application remains in unauthenticated state.
The application behavior for leading whitespace in credentials is consistently observable, and if authentication fails the user remains unauthenticated with only the generic Failed message displayed.

The application behavior for trailing whitespace in credentials is consistently observable, and failed authentication does not transition the application into authenticated state while displaying only the generic Failed message.
Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation appears, and the application remains unauthenticated.
The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state and authenticated-only UI is not visible.
The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state and authenticated-only features are not displayed.
The application remains unauthenticated, authenticated user identity is not displayed, authenticated-only features remain inaccessible, and only the generic Failed message is shown.
The pasted approved credentials authenticate successfully and the application returns to authenticated home_page with authenticated user identity and authenticated-only UI visible.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.

unresolvedGaps: The Login page Username field is missing from the approved elements despite being a core workflow interaction element.
The Login page Password field is missing from the approved elements even though password entry and masking validation are core workflow requirements.
The Login button is missing from the approved elements, preventing downstream authentication workflow automation.
No generic Failed authentication message element exists even though the workflow explicitly requires validation of the Failed message across all negative authentication scenarios.
The generic anchor element duplicates the Home button ownership already modeled by auto_home_btn_button and uses an unsafe locator.
Locator xpath=//a is extremely generic and likely to match multiple links, making the element brittle and unsafe for automation reuse.
The background content element is decorative/layout-only and provides no meaningful workflow automation value.
The element name is technical, implementation-centric, and not business-readable.
The element name exposes raw DOM naming instead of business intent.
The element name is implementation-centric and weak for reusable automation generation.
The element name is tied to DOM naming conventions instead of user-facing semantics.
The element name is inconsistent and overly technical.
The element name is implementation-oriented instead of workflow-oriented.
The element name is not business-readable and weak for downstream keyword generation.
Customer signup is an upstream home-page workflow navigation control and does not belong to the canonical Login page model.
Clean survey is an upstream home-page workflow navigation control unrelated to Login page ownership.
Site survey is an upstream home-page workflow navigation control unrelated to Login page ownership.
